headline: Lithium DOUBLES In Q1 2026 — Battery-Grade Carbonate Up 95% To $26,278/t As Storage Hits 31% Of Demand
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate nearly doubled in Q1 2026, rising 95% to $26,278/tonne. Energy storage applications now account for 31% of total lithium consumption — up from 23% in 2025 — with CATL supply disruptions at its Jianxiawo mine compounding the price squeeze.

headline: NexGen Energy STARTS ROOK Construction — Canada's Largest Uranium Deposit Breaks Ground As AI Supercharges Demand
snippet: NexGen Energy has started construction at its Rook uranium mine in Saskatchewan — Canada's largest development-stage uranium deposit. A global investor survey finds that AI hyperscale data centres are reshaping long-term nuclear demand expectations, with most respondents expecting mined uranium to cover less than 75% of future reactor needs.

headline: Mining M&A HITS 13-YEAR HIGH — $93.7B In 2025 Deals Sets Stage For Continued 2026 Consolidation
snippet: Global mining M&A activity reached $93.7 billion in 2025 — the highest yearly total since 2012 — as elevated gold prices created powerful acquisition currency and demand for critical minerals intensified strategic competition. Gold remained the most targeted commodity.

headline: K92 Mining Posts 46,743 oz AuEq In Q1 2026 — Kainantu Keeps Pace As Gold Margins Widen To Record $2,800+/oz
snippet: K92 Mining (TSX-V: KNT) delivered Q1 2026 production of 46,743 gold-equivalent ounces from its Kainantu Mine in Papua New Guinea, keeping the junior producer on a strong annual pace while spot gold margins hit record levels above $2,800/oz.

headline: China Northern Rare Earth HIKES Q2 Prices 44% — Export Controls Already Forcing Downstream Buyers To Scramble
snippet: China Northern Rare Earth Group set Q2 2026 concentrate prices at 38,804 yuan (~$5,390/tonne), a 44% increase. The hike is a direct market consequence of China's Announcement 18 export control regime, with downstream manufacturers outside China reporting urgent scrambles for alternative supply.

headline: US Copper TARIFF DEADLINE Looms — Commerce Dept Must Rule By June 30 On Potential 15-30% Duty From 2027
snippet: The US Secretary of Commerce must deliver an update on the domestic copper market by June 30, 2026 under a Section 232 review — potentially recommending a 15% tariff on refined copper from 2027, rising to 30% by 2028. The decision could reshape global copper trade flows and accelerate domestic mine permitting.

headline: REalloys Locks In MONTANA Rare Earth Supply — 10% Of Sheep Creek Output Secured Ahead Of 2027 Defense Cutoff
snippet: US rare earth processor REalloys signed an MOU with US Critical Materials Corp to secure up to 10% of production from the Sheep Creek rare earth deposit in Montana. The deal positions REalloys ahead of 2027 US defense rules that require the removal of Chinese-sourced rare earth materials.

headline: Newmont Q1 Earnings Approach — Stock At $120 Trades At Record Discount To Gold With Margins At All-Time Highs
snippet: Newmont (NYSE: NEM) trades near $120 at just 12x forward earnings — well below its five-year average of 16x — despite reporting 2025 EPS of $6.89 (up 98% year-over-year). Motley Fool analysts argue the world's largest gold miner is mispriced ahead of Q1 results with margins at record levels.

headline: The Gold Trend NOBODY Is Talking About In 2026 — Canadian Mining Report Flags Overlooked Catalyst For Junior Rally
snippet: Canadian Mining Report identifies a structural gold market trend that retail investors are broadly missing — with junior and mid-tier miners still trading at multi-year discounts to NAV despite record bullion prices, the setup for a catch-up trade may be closer than the consensus acknowledges.

headline: BloombergNEF: SUPPLY CHAINS STRUGGLING To Keep Pace With Energy Transition Metal Demand — Deficits Widening
snippet: A new BloombergNEF report finds that supply chains for critical transition metals — copper, lithium, cobalt, nickel — are structurally unable to keep pace with surging demand from EVs, grid storage, and AI data centres. Projected deficits are widening across most battery and wiring metals through the decade.
